mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above prior month by > 0.2pp
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days exclusively during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns during recovery phases or shows no directional edge (Rank IC < 0.01) when filtered by NBER expansion start dates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation months, or inflation series shows zero predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units signal strong residential investment demand that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months where housing starts cross the 1.2 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 50 bps annually, or the interaction effect reverses sign during labor market stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining monthly retail sales signal weakening consumer spending that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days exclusively during late-cycle expansion regimes.
failure_condition: Signal generates positive forward returns during late-cycle phases or shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) when isolated to NBER-dated late expansion periods.
